Repository: opencv/opencv
Release: OpenCV 4.3.0
Tag: 4.3.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-04-03T22:42:41Z; created_at: 2020-04-03T11:45:28Z
Release notes:
OpenCV 4.3.0 has been released.
:hammer_and_wrench: Change log is [here](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/wiki/ChangeLog#version430).
:star: Introducing new Windows package: OpenCV DNN accelerated by Inference Engine (DLDT) ([Wiki page](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/wiki/Intel%27s-Deep-Learning-Inference-Engine-backend))
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the opencv/opencv release 4.3.0, the project announces the release of OpenCV 4.3.0, introduces a new Windows package with OpenCV DNN accelerated by Inference Engine (DLDT), and provides a change log link.

questions.query_text: In the opencv/opencv releases, which release introduces a new Windows package with OpenCV DNN accelerated by Inference Engine (DLDT)?

answer_support.answer_text: introduces a new Windows package with OpenCV DNN accelerated by Inference Engine (DLDT)

questions.query_text: Across the opencv/opencv releases, how does the package availability described in OpenCV 4.9.0 differ from the package availability described in OpenCV 4.8.1?

options.text: 4.9.0 adds Android AAR availability on Maven Central and includes docs package, while 4.8.1 does not mention Maven Central and lacks docs package.
4.9.0 removes iOS framework support, while 4.8.1 includes it.
Both releases provide identical package sets with no differences.
4.9.0 only provides Windows executable, while 4.8.1 provides Android, iOS, and Windows.

questions.expected_answer: 4.9.0 adds Android AAR availability on Maven Central and includes docs package, while 4.8.1 does not mention Maven Central and lacks docs package.

answer_support.answer_text: Android package is also available as AAR on Maven Central starting from this version, and lists SHA256 sums for Android SDK, iOS framework, Windows executable, and docs.
lists SHA256 sums for Android SDK, iOS framework, and Windows executable.

questions.query_text: In the opencv/opencv release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that provides SHA256 sums for Android SDK, docs, iOS framework, and Windows packages, (2) a release that introduces a new Windows package with OpenCV DNN accelerated by Inference Engine (DLDT), (3) a release that announces the alpha release of OpenCV 5.0.0 as a technology preview not ready for production.

answer_support.answer_text: introduces a new Windows package with OpenCV DNN accelerated by Inference Engine (DLDT)
provides SHA256 sums for the Android SDK, docs, iOS framework, and Windows packages
announces the alpha release of OpenCV 5.0.0 as a technology preview not ready for production
